name: Lijiang
intro: Lijiang suits travelers who want Yunnan to open with beauty, softness, and a little altitude without immediately becoming severe, especially when the real goal is to enter the highlands through water, wood, sunlight, and slower breathing rather than through pure difficulty.
seoTitle: Lijiang Travel Guide for First-Time Visitors
seoDescription: Plan a first trip to Lijiang with practical advice on route fit, stay length, old-town pacing, and when Yunnan works best by beginning in the softer highlands.
tripPace: Best when the stay stays edited: one old-town rhythm, one mountain-or-view day, and enough slack for altitude, weather, and the simple act of letting Yunnan arrive slowly.
highlights: One of the clearest ways to enter Yunnan without making the highlands feel punishing from the first night.
Strongest when travelers understand Lijiang as a threshold city: beautiful, commercial, theatrical, but still genuinely useful as the first breath before the route rises higher.
Usually works best when old-town texture, mountain light, and route pacing matter more than chasing a full checklist of scenic side trips.

planningPriorities.title: Decide whether Lijiang is the opening or the destination
planningPriorities.description: Lijiang can work as a full stay, but for many first-time visitors it is most persuasive as the gentler first chapter in a wider Yunnan branch.

planningPriorities.title: Protect softness before adding altitude pressure
planningPriorities.description: The city usually improves once travelers let the old town, mountain views, and slower first day do their work before pushing deeper into the highlands.

planningPriorities.title: Choose one strong mountain branch, not all of them
planningPriorities.description: Jade Dragon Snow Mountain and the wider plateau scenery can easily turn the stay into a transport puzzle if the route stops editing itself.

transportNote: Lijiang works best once the Yunnan branch is honest about what comes next, because its airport, train links, and altitude all make more sense when the city is treated as a real opening base rather than just a decorative overnight.
heroImageAlt: The tightly layered tiled rooftops of Lijiang Old Town spreading across the basin under clear plateau light.
